analyst_finding: DUPLICATE_OF_ISS-705 — W004/WIN-025 mapping already verified; results.json endpoint now 404

prior_investigation.conclusion: W004 in results.json IS the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication test. The W-numbering scheme in results.json differs from the wins-page WIN-numbering. W004 in results.json = our WIN-025 (eclipse test), which is NOT the same as wins-page WIN-004 (Noise Floor Limit). Identity mapping confirmed by two independent fetches (2026-04-10).

new_finding_results_json_removed.api_index_listing: results.json NOT listed in api/index.json discovery document as of 2026-04-11
new_finding_results_json_removed.implication: The dome has removed the results.json endpoint entirely. The file that was live at /api/current/results.json on 2026-04-09 (35 entries, 100% pass rate) and confirmed present on 2026-04-10 is now gone. Our section 2b.7 references data from a now-defunct endpoint. The data was captured at fetch time and the field dumps in EXP-055 and ISS-705-verification.json preserve the evidence. The removal is itself significant — the dome site removed the structured scoring endpoint after our review documented its contradictions.

conclusion.recommended_action: Close ISS-715 as duplicate of ISS-705 (already fixed). Additionally, consider creating a new issue for the results.json removal — section 2b.7 should note that the endpoint was removed after our analysis, with a parenthetical like "(results.json was live at the time of our fetch 2026-04-09; the endpoint has since been removed, circa 2026-04-10 to 2026-04-11)". Our cached evidence in ISS-705-verification.json preserves the field dumps.

new_issue_suggested.title: SEC-2b.7 STALE ENDPOINT: results.json now returns 404 — add temporal note
new_issue_suggested.description: The /api/current/results.json endpoint referenced in section 2b.7 now returns 404. The dome appears to have removed it between 2026-04-10 and 2026-04-11. Section 2b.7 should add a parenthetical noting the fetch date and that the endpoint was subsequently removed. The removal is itself evidence supporting the section's thesis — the dome responded to scoring criticism by removing the scoreboard rather than fixing the scoring rules.
